style_stack.visual: broll_montage PRIMARY (matrix=static plates+hard cuts; smoke keeps Ken Burns)

beats.speakable_text: If a skipped glance at the banking app is familiar, this financial anxiety pattern needs a better reset.
Trying to outrun financial anxiety usually makes the loop more expensive. More avoidance of the numbers, more impulsive fixes. And less of an actual plan.

Financial anxiety often persists because money gets processed as a safety issue, not a math issue. So the threat response fires before the numbers do. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.
Try this. Open the app, write down the one true number. And decide only the next single action. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.
Bookmark the pattern. Signal, reset, next action. A plan you can look at is calmer than a fear you can't.
